content: We can model a curiosity-driven autonomous agent’s active memory updating after the prefrontal-hippocampal interaction that underpins biological retrieval, by integrating a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) memory hierarchy where curiosity signals from the agent’s intrinsic motivation module (like ICM or RND) actively prioritize which existing memory entries to re-retrieve and refine for long-term storage, rather than only appending new information. This creates a neurally-aligned curious agent that forgets low-value outdated memories and consolidates high-uncertainty relevant memories, solving the common problem of stale, cluttered agent memory that plagues current 2024-2025 SOTA active learning agent architectures.
